informal: Let $G$ be a transitive permutation group on the finite set $A$ with $|A|>1$. Show that there is some $\sigma \in G$ such that $\sigma(a) \neq a$ for all $a \in A$.
lean:
theorem Dummit_Foote_exercise_4_3_26_corrected {α : Type*} [Fintype α] (ha : Fintype.card α > 1)
  (G : Subgroup (Equiv.Perm α))
  (h_tran : ∀ a b : α, ∃ σ : G, (σ : Equiv.Perm α) a = b) :
  ∃ σ : G, ∀ a : α, (σ : Equiv.Perm α) a ≠ a
reasoning: The Lean signature captures the informal claim precisely: α is a finite type with |α|>1, G is a subgroup of Perm α (a permutation group on α), and h_tran encodes transitivity as the standard "for all a, b there exists σ ∈ G with σ(a)=b". The conclusion ∃ σ ∈ G, ∀ a, σ a ≠ a is exactly the existence of a fixed-point-free element. The quantifier structure, hypotheses, and ambient objects match the informal statement, so this is a strictly faithful formalization.
